revision.comment: 新しいページ：コンピラ山古墳（奈良県大和高田市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''コンピラ山古墳'''（こんぴらやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の東側に築造された大型円墳である（かつては築山古墳の[[陪塚]]と想定された）。現在までに墳丘裾部が削平を受けているほか、近年に範囲確認の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 墳形は円形で、直径95メートル・高さ12.7メートルを測り（発掘調査以前は直径55メートルとされた）{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}、奈良県内の円墳としては[[富雄丸山古墳]]（[[奈良市]]、直径109メートル）に次ぐ規模になる。墳丘は2段築成[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html コンピラ山古墳]（大和高田市ホームページ）。。墳丘表面ではテラス部・墳頂部で[[円筒埴輪]]列が検出されているほか、墳頂部からは盾形・家形埴輪が、墳丘斜面からは蓋形埴輪が出土している{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。特に円筒埴輪のうち2本の中からはミニチュア土器が出土した点で注目される（類例は[[乙女山古墳]]・[[渋谷向山古墳]]などで知られる）。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期前半の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|コンピラ古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2002|chapter=コンピラ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|コンピラ古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1991|title= コンピラ山古墳 第1次発掘調査概報|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1992|title= コンピラ山古墳 第2次発掘調査概報|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1993|title= コンピラ山古墳 第3次発掘調査報告書|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1995|title= コンピラ山古墳 第4次発掘調査報告書|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[築山古墳 (大和高田市)]]（磐園陵墓参考地） == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html コンピラ山古墳] - 大和高田市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:こんひらやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:大和高田市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''コンピラ山古墳'''（こんぴらやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の東側に築造された大型円墳である（かつては築山古墳の[[陪塚]]と想定された）。現在までに墳丘裾部が削平を受けているほか、近年に範囲確認の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 墳形は円形で、直径95メートル・高さ12.7メートルを測り（発掘調査以前は直径55メートルとされた）{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}、奈良県内の円墳としては[[富雄丸山古墳]]（[[奈良市]]、直径109メートル）に次ぐ規模になる。墳丘は2段築成[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html コンピラ山古墳]（大和高田市ホームページ）。。墳丘表面ではテラス部・墳頂部で[[円筒埴輪]]列が検出されているほか、墳頂部からは盾形・家形埴輪が、墳丘斜面からは蓋形埴輪が出土している{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。特に円筒埴輪のうち2本の中からはミニチュア土器が出土した点で注目される（類例は[[乙女山古墳]]・[[渋谷向山古墳]]などで知られる）。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期前半の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|コンピラ古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2002|chapter=コンピラ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|コンピラ古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1991|title= コンピラ山古墳 第1次発掘調査概報|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1992|title= コンピラ山古墳 第2次発掘調査概報|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1993|title= コンピラ山古墳 第3次発掘調査報告書|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1995|title= コンピラ山古墳 第4次発掘調査報告書|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[築山古墳 (大和高田市)]]（磐園陵墓参考地） == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html コンピラ山古墳] - 大和高田市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:こんひらやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:大和高田市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''コンピラ山古墳'''（こんぴらやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の東側に築造された大型円墳である（かつては築山古墳の[[陪塚]]と想定された）。現在までに墳丘裾部が削平を受けているほか、近年に範囲確認の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 墳形は円形で、直径95メートル・高さ12.7メートルを測り（発掘調査以前は直径55メートルとされた）{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}、奈良県内の円墳としては[[富雄丸山古墳]]（[[奈良市]]、直径109メートル）に次ぐ規模になる。墳丘は2段築成[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html コンピラ山古墳]（大和高田市ホームページ）。。墳丘表面ではテラス部・墳頂部で[[円筒埴輪]]列が検出されているほか、墳頂部からは盾形・家形埴輪が、墳丘斜面からは蓋形埴輪が出土している{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。特に円筒埴輪のうち2本の中からはミニチュア土器が出土した点で注目される（類例は[[乙女山古墳]]・[[渋谷向山古墳]]などで知られる）。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 このコンピラ山古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期前半の[[5世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|コンピラ古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。馬見古墳群では、[[巣山古墳]]・[[乙女山古墳]]、[[新木山古墳]]・[[三吉石塚古墳]]、倉塚古墳・三吉古墳、[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]・コンピラ山古墳のように大型前方後円墳と帆立貝形古墳（または大型円墳）が近接するという特徴が認められており、[[ヒメヒコ制]]との関連性を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2002|chapter=コンピラ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|コンピラ古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite コンピラ山古墳 第2次発掘調査概報|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]編|chapter=|year=1993|title= コンピラ山古墳 第3次発掘調査報告書|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]・コンピラ山古墳のように大型前方後円墳と帆立貝形古墳（または大型円墳）が近接するという特徴が認められており、[[ヒメヒコ制]]との関連性を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''コンピラ山古墳'''（こんぴらやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の東側に築造された大型円墳である（かつては築山古墳の[[陪塚]]と想定された）。現在までに墳丘裾部が削平を受けているほか、近年に範囲確認の発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。 墳形は円形で、直径95メートル・高さ12.7メートルを測り（発掘調査以前は直径55メートルとされた）{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}、奈良県内の円墳としては[[富雄丸山古墳]]（[[奈良市]]、直径109メートル）に次ぐ規模になる。墳丘は2段築成[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html コンピラ山古墳]（大和高田市ホームページ）。。墳丘表面ではテラス部・墳頂部で[[円筒埴輪]]列が検出されているほか、墳頂部からは盾形・家形埴輪が、墳丘斜面からは蓋形埴輪が出土している{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。特に円筒埴輪のうち2本の中からはミニチュア土器が出土した点で注目される（類例は[[乙女山古墳]]・[[渋谷向山古墳]]などで知られる）。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}。埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 book|和書|editor=|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada]] [[Category:Circular Kofun]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources ==
