{{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == 高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular burial mound (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular burial mound built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January [[Category:Konpira Yama Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Konpira Yama Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website 墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern part_of within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == 墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |ISO = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == 墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. Artifacts such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designation1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == 墳丘 |別名 = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 24.70 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[円墳]] |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |埋葬施設 = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (other_designation1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources ==
