revision.text:
[[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.52352777777778|N|135.73388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109364414]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{translated page|ja|コンピラ山古墳|version=98271537|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:%E3%82%B3%E3%83%B3%E3%83%94%E3%83%A9%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada City]] 墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.52352777777778|N|135.73388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109364414]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural 高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.52352777777778|N|135.73388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109364414]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.52352777777778|N|135.73388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109364414]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada City}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1993|title=Third Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) == External Links == * [http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' {{ill|Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpira Yama Kofun|lt=Konpirayama Kofun|ja|コンピラ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpirayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamatotakada, circular, diameter 95 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January {{translated page|ja|コンピラ山古墳|version=98271537|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:%E3%82%B3%E3%83%B3%E3%83%94%E3%83%A9%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q109364414}} Kofun]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.52352777777778|N|135.73388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada City}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1991|title=First Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1992|title=Second Excavation Report on Konpirayama Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Kofun|publisher=Yamatotakada City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|editor-link=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|chapter=|year=1995|title=Fourth The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2002|chapter=Konpira Kofun|title=[[Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvnb|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=The Great Kofun Clusters of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Not directly used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Nara Prefectural [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] [[ja:コンピラ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.52352777777778|N|135.73388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = コンピラ山古墳 |image = [[File:Konpirayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |alternate_name = コンピラ古墳 |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]築山字今屋敷 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 24.70 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 1.95 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = 直径95m
高さ12.7m |Burial Facility = 不明 |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |built = [[5世紀]]前半 |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = コンピラ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada City|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''Konpirayama Kofun''' (コンピラ山古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|qid=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada City|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a circular Kofun (円墳) and part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Group within the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). This kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster}}{{small|{{National Aerial Photograph}}}}]] The Konpirayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun built to the east of {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as the Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) at the southern end of the Makimuku Hills in the western Nara Basin. Previously considered an {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mound) of the Tsukiyama Kofun, recent boundary-confirmation excavations have been conducted, revealing its true significance.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The mound is circular, measuring 95 meters in diameter and 12.7 meters in height (prior to excavations, it was thought to be 55 meters in diameter).{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} It is the second-largest circular kofun in Nara Prefecture, following the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28688548}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} (109 meters in diameter). The mound consists of two tiers.[http://www.city.yamatotakada.nara.jp/city/rekishi/iseki/kofun/konpira.html Konpirayama Kofun] - Yamatotakada City Official Website. burial goods such as cylindrical haniwa were found on the terraces and mound summit, including shield-shaped and house-shaped haniwa from the summit and lid-shaped haniwa from the slopes.{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} Miniature pottery was discovered inside two cylindrical haniwa, similar to finds at other notable kofun such as {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|qid=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}} and {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat. However, its burial facilities and grave goods remain unknown. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period).{{Sfn|Konpira Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Kawakami Kunihiko|2006}} The Makimuku Kofun Cluster features a notable pattern where large keyhole-shaped mounds and circular mounds (or scallop-shell mounds) are constructed in close proximity. This arrangement has been linked to the {{ill|Hime-Hiko System|ja|ヒメヒコ制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-Hiko System|qq=}}, a governance structure based on dual leadership.{{Sfn|Kawakami
